title: basananthe malaissei
intro: Basananthe malaissei, also known as the African spurge or bushman's poison, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its toxic latex and milky sap. The plant contains compounds with medicinal properties, including cardenolides, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Basananthe malaissei include its potential use in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as a purgative and emetic. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its latex is sometimes used to treat wounds and skin irritations.
